title: Cleavable Cross-Linkers Redefined by a Novel MS<sup>3</sup>-Trigger Algorithm.
author_string: Kolbowski, Lars, Fischer, Lutz, Rappsilber, Juri
abstract: Cross-linking mass spectrometry (MS) is currently transitioning from a routine tool in structural biology to enabling structural systems biology. MS-cleavable cross-linkers could substantially reduce the associated search space expansion by allowing a MS<sup>3</sup>-based approach for identifying cross-linked peptides. However, MS<sup>2</sup> (MS/MS)-based approaches currently outperform approaches utilizing MS<sup>3</sup>. We show here that the sensitivity and specificity of triggering MS<sup>3</sup> have been hampered algorithmically. Our four-step MS<sup>3</sup>-trigger algorithm greatly outperformed currently employed methods and comes close to reaching the theoretical limit.
